title: Cloud/account pivot: server access → harvested secret
impact: Compromise of external systems the leaked credential unlocks
advice: Break the chain at step 1 — parameterise the query / stop running attacker-controlled input (app/api/products/route.ts:7). Fixing that one thing stops this whole path.
score_factors: chain reaches a concrete impact (data/system compromise)
exposes a credential — blast radius beyond this app
2 distinct weaknesses align into one chain

steps.title: Server-side code/query execution
steps.narrative: User input flows into a database query (app/api/products/route.ts:7) can run code or queries on the server.

steps.title: A real secret is sitting right there
steps.narrative: AWS access key committed to the repository (.env.local:2) puts a live credential within reach of that primitive.

steps.title: Pivot beyond the app with stolen keys
steps.narrative: With the secret in hand, the attacker authenticates to your cloud / database / third-party services — the blast radius now extends past this app.

title: Build/CI compromise via a malicious dependency
impact: Code execution on your build server, CI, or developers' machines
advice: Break the chain at step 1 — remove or pin this dependency and audit the script (package.json:8). Fixing that one thing stops this whole path.
score_factors: chain reaches a concrete impact (data/system compromise)

steps.title: Malicious code arrives via a dependency
steps.narrative: Dangerous install lifecycle script in package.json (package.json:8) — Lifecycle script 'postinstall': Pipes remote content directly into a shell/interpreter.

steps.title: It runs at install time, before your app starts
steps.narrative: npm runs lifecycle scripts on `npm install` / CI — this executes on your build server or developer machine with no app code involved.

title: Injection via untrusted input → paint() -> el.innerHTML [in lib/render.ts]
advice: Break the chain at step 2 — validate or parameterise the value at the sink (app/api/products/route.ts:10). Fixing that one thing stops this whole path.
score_factors: chain reaches a concrete impact (data/system compromise)

steps.title: Attacker controls untrusted input
steps.narrative: An attacker supplies a value via `req.body` (app/api/products/route.ts:5) reaching app/api/products/route.ts. Nothing about this input is trusted.

steps.title: Untrusted input reaches a dangerous sink
steps.narrative: That value flows unsanitised into `paint() -> el.innerHTML [in lib/render.ts]` (app/api/products/route.ts:10) — the attacker now controls what the server runs.

title: Exploitation of lodash via CVE-2021-23337
impact: Compromise via a publicly-known dependency exploit
advice: Break the chain at step 1 — upgrade lodash to 4.17.21 (package.json:14). Fixing that one thing stops this whole path.
score_factors: chain reaches a concrete impact (data/system compromise)

steps.title: Exposed dependency with a public exploit
steps.narrative: You ship `lodash` affected by CVE-2021-23337, reachable from code you actually call.

steps.title: Attacker triggers the known vulnerability
steps.narrative: Public exploit code exists; the attacker runs it against your deployment.
